feature_name: Restrict rotation CronJob ServiceAccount to read-only
summary: The RBAC split, annotation-gated commit, validation contract, envtest wiring, chainsaw assertions, and operator docs are all in good shape and match the CC-0081 contract. However, the apply step in rotation_staging.go:113-126 is built on a false premise about strategic-merge-patch semantics. A strategic-merge patch on Secret.Data (a map[string][]byte with no patchStrategy tag) merges keys rather than replacing the map; stale keys in the production Secret that are absent from the staging payload are preserved indefinitely. An empirical reproduction against the real strategicpatch library confirmed this (a pre-existing key '3' survived a patch that staged only {0,1,2}). Because keystone-manage fernet_rotate renumbers key files across rotations and max_active_keys reductions prune indices, this bug means the production key set will monotonically grow on every rotation in the typical steady state. Every existing test masks the bug by using key indices that are a subset of the staging indices, so neither rotation_staging_apply_test.go nor the envtest end-to-end exercise the disjoint-index case.

issues_found.description: Strategic-merge patch on Secret.Data does not replace the map — stale production keys are preserved across rotations. The DECISION comment claims 'strategic-merge on map-typed fields replaces the map cleanly', but Secret.Data is `map[string][]byte` with no `patchStrategy` tag (verified in k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go:7892), so strategic-merge patch defaults to merge-by-key. An empirical reproduction using k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/strategicpatch.StrategicMergePatch with the exact payload shape the operator emits produced {0,1,2,3} when the original was {0,1,2,3-stale} and the patch was {0,1,2}. Over repeated rotations — or a decrease in spec.fernet.maxActiveKeys / spec.credentialKeys.maxActiveKeys — stale keys will accumulate in the production Secret, silently violating REQ-006's bounded-keys contract and leaving decommissioned fernet keys usable for token validation. This undermines the whole point of the rotation pipeline.
issues_found.evidence:
Line 114: `patch, marshalErr := json.Marshal(map[string]map[string][]byte{"data": staging.Data})`
Line 124: `r.Patch(ctx, mainSecret, client.RawPatch(types.StrategicMergePatchType, patch))`
Lines 70-73: `client.RawPatch(StrategicMergePatchType) with {"data": {...}} is preferred over client.MergeFrom because strategic-merge on map-typed fields replaces the map cleanly` — factually incorrect.
Empirical reproduction: StrategicMergePatch(orig, patch, Secret{}) with orig.Data={0,1,2,3-stale} and patch.data={0,1,2} returns {0:new0, 1:new1, 2:new2, 3:old3-stale}.
issues_found.fix: Replace the strategic-merge patch with either (a) a full Update after GET: fetch the main Secret, assign staging.Data verbatim, Update; or (b) a JSON merge patch that explicitly sets Data to the new map after a GET-and-compute-null-for-missing-keys step; or (c) a Server-Side Apply with the operator as the field manager of `.data` and `Replace` semantics. Option (a) is the simplest and matches the 'atomic swap' intent the DECISION comment was reaching for; the extra GET is cheap and the optimistic-concurrency retry is already shape-compatible with the rest of the reconcile. Then add a regression test that seeds production with keys at indices disjoint from staging (e.g. prod={5,6}, staging={0,1,2}) and asserts len(prod.Data)==3 after apply, to lock the semantics in. Once fixed, also delete the misleading DECISION comment block on lines 70-73.

issues_found.description: Both the unit test for applyRotationOutput and the envtest end-to-end test only exercise staging/production key sets whose indices overlap fully, so they cannot observe the merge-vs-replace bug in S1. This is the reason the misreading of strategic-merge semantics has reached review. Any regression test landing alongside a fix for S1 must seed the production Secret with at least one key index that does not appear in the staging payload and assert the production Data length equals the staging Data length — otherwise the same blind spot will re-open the same class of bug on the next refactor.
issues_found.evidence:
rotation_staging_apply_test.go:294-297: production seeded with `{"0": []byte("existing")}`, staging has 3 keys at "0","1","2" — "0" is overwritten by the merge and no disjoint key exists.
integration_test.go:2042-2047: staging is populated with keys at indices "0","1","2" generated fresh; production was just initialized by createFernetKeysSecret with normalizedFernetMaxActiveKeys==3 keys at the same indices "0","1","2". No test path places a key at an index that the staging payload omits.
issues_found.fix: Add (at minimum) one unit test case in rotation_staging_apply_test.go and one envtest case that seed the production Secret with a key at an index outside the staging indices (e.g. production Data = {"7": oldBytes}, staging Data = valid 3-key set at {0,1,2} with completion annotation) and then assert gotProd.Data has exactly the staging keys and length — not four keys. This test must fail against the current implementation (confirming the S1 bug) and pass after the fix.

issues_found.description: The DECISION comment block in rotation_staging.go:63-73 bundles two independent decisions (PATCH-then-DELETE ordering + strategic-merge-patch choice) under a single header, and the second one is wrong (see S1). Readers who trust the comment will propagate the misconception into future changes — e.g. to rotation of any other `map[string][]byte` Secret.
issues_found.evidence: `DECISION (CC-0081): client.RawPatch(StrategicMergePatchType) with {"data": {...}} is preferred over client.MergeFrom because strategic-merge on map-typed fields replaces the map cleanly, which matches the intended "atomic swap" semantics of the rotation apply step.`
issues_found.fix: After fixing S1, remove the second DECISION paragraph entirely. If the operator ends up using Update (option (a) in S1's fix), the remaining comment should be one short line explaining that the apply performs a full data replacement under a controller-owned ResourceVersion to guarantee atomic swap of the active key set, nothing more.

issues_found.description: ensureFernetStagingSecret (reconcile_fernet.go:216-240) and ensureCredentialStagingSecret (reconcile_credential.go:236-260) are near-verbatim duplicates differing only in the staging-name helper and the label value. The rotation_staging.go file already owns the staging-Secret primitives (label key, naming helpers) and is the natural home for a shared helper. Leaving both copies invites skew — e.g. a future fix to one (label added, annotation tolerated, owner-ref pattern changed) that doesn't propagate to the other. This is exactly the kind of 'change one, forget the other' trap CC-0073 called out for the rotation scripts.
issues_found.evidence: Both functions: CreateOrUpdate(secret), label = commonLabels + StagingSecretLabelKey:"<fernet|credential>-keys", no Data touch, SetControllerReference. Only differences: the variable name flavor and the label value string.
issues_found.fix: Introduce an unexported helper `ensureStagingSecret(ctx, keystone, name, labelValue string) error` in rotation_staging.go; call it from reconcile_fernet.go and reconcile_credential.go. Keep the call-site labels distinct so the per-kind label value stays grep-visible at each caller.

issues_found.description: applyRotationOutput swallows the completion-annotation-missing case silently (returns false, nil with no event) but emits a Warning event for the malformed-annotation case. From a human-operator UX angle these are symmetric failure modes — 'CronJob ran but didn't commit'. Silent no-op on missing annotation is correct for the steady state between rotations (staging Secret intentionally empty and unannotated), but it's indistinguishable from a CronJob that wrote Data without the annotation, which is a real bug class (earlier drafts of the script had this exact shape). Consider emitting a single debug-level log line (not an event — events would be noisy) when staging.Data is non-empty AND the annotation is missing, so operators debugging a stuck rotation can tell 'CronJob forgot to annotate' from 'CronJob hasn't run yet'.
issues_found.evidence: Lines 94-98: if annotation absent or empty string, immediately `return false, nil` — no log line distinguishes 'empty staging Secret (normal)' from 'populated staging Secret without annotation (script bug)'.
issues_found.fix: Before the early return on missing annotation, add a single `log.V(1).Info(...)` when `len(staging.Data) > 0` calling out the 'staged data without completion annotation' case. Keep it at verbosity 1 so it doesn't spam normal operation. No event, no requeue-with-error.

issues_found.description: The rotation_staging_apply_test.go HappyPath comment at line 293 (`map-typed data field replaces cleanly`) reinforces the same misconception as the DECISION block from S1/Q2. Even after the S1 fix, this comment would remain in the test suite and mislead anyone reading the test as a specification. Cleaning it up alongside the S1/Q2 fix keeps the codebase narratively consistent.
issues_found.evidence: Line 293: `// Production starts with a single key that must be replaced by the // strategic merge patch (map-typed data field replaces cleanly).`
issues_found.fix: After the S1 fix, delete the parenthetical `(map-typed data field replaces cleanly)` and, if the apply switches away from strategic-merge-patch, also drop the `strategic merge patch` phrase in the preceding sentence so the comment describes behavior, not mechanism.

issues_found.description: applyRotationOutput computes `len(staging.Data)` after the strategic-merge patch in the success event message, which gives a misleading count once S1 is fixed (and is currently meaningless given the merge-vs-replace bug). The count the operator cares about — and that ops expect in the event — is 'how many active keys are now in production'. If the fix for S1 moves to Update, the value the event should carry is the new Data length, which equals staging.Data length at that moment; either way, make the intent explicit in the message so a reader scanning events doesn't mistake 'keys staged' for 'keys in production'.
issues_found.evidence: Line 135: `"rotation applied from staging secret %s (%d keys)", stagingSecretName, len(staging.Data)`
issues_found.fix: After the S1 fix, either (a) rephrase as `"rotation applied from staging secret %s (%d active keys)"` to make the semantic explicit, or (b) re-GET the production Secret post-apply and report `len(gotProd.Data)` so the count is observed rather than asserted. (a) is lower cost and adequate.

issues_found.description: The envtest `TestRotationApplyEndToEnd_EnvTest` simulates the CronJob by calling `c.Update(ctx, staging)` with the new Data and annotation in one shot. The real CronJob uses a strategic-merge PATCH with only `data` + `annotations` subtrees and runs as a distinct ServiceAccount subject to the split RBAC. The envtest therefore exercises the operator's apply path but not the CronJob's actual write shape, which is exactly where the `strategic-merge preserves keys not in the patch` semantic first bites. Consequently the test passes even though the CronJob-side round-trip of this same PATCH shape (applied to the staging Secret as starting state) would behave differently in real clusters. Worth covering the CronJob's PATCH behavior explicitly so the end-to-end coverage is complete.
issues_found.evidence: Line 2054: `g.Expect(c.Update(ctx, staging)).To(Succeed(), "stage CronJob output onto staging Secret (CC-0081)")` — full-object Update, not the strategic-merge PATCH the production script emits.
issues_found.fix: Either (a) simulate the CronJob side with a Patch call using StrategicMergePatchType and the exact payload shape from fernet_rotate.sh (json with `metadata.annotations` + `data` subtrees), or (b) add a focused unit test that feeds the real patch payload into fakeclient and asserts the resulting staging Secret shape. (a) closes the gap end-to-end; (b) is quicker.

security_findings.rbac_evidence: reconcile_fernet.go:165-184 and reconcile_credential.go:185-204 each define two PolicyRules: (1) APIGroups [''] Resources ['secrets'] Verbs ['get'] ResourceNames [<production-name>], (2) APIGroups [''] Resources ['secrets'] Verbs ['get','patch'] ResourceNames [<staging-name>]. No create/delete on either Secret; no cluster-wide rule. Chainsaw fernet-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml:196-224 and credential-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml:149-191 assert the absence of patch on the production name and presence of patch on the staging name end-to-end.
security_findings.validation_contract_complete: rotation_validation.go:40-74 enforces count in [minKeys,maxKeys], 44-byte values decoding via base64.URLEncoding to 32 bytes, pairwise distinctness, with deterministic error messages under sorted iteration. All paths wrap sentinel errors for errors.Is matching.
security_findings.production_write_caveat: The operator's write via strategic-merge PATCH does not fully replace production.Data (see S1); so although the CronJob cannot write production, the operator's write is semantically the wrong kind of replace for this use case.

architecture_findings.staging_secret_lifecycle: Operator owns metadata (labels, owner reference) and creation/deletion; CronJob owns .data field via narrow get+patch RBAC. ensureFernetStagingSecret and ensureCredentialStagingSecret deliberately do not touch secret.Data in the CreateOrUpdate mutator (reconcile_fernet.go:233, reconcile_credential.go:253), so operator reconciles never clobber staged keys.
architecture_findings.annotation_gating_correct: rotation_staging.go:94-104 requires non-empty RotationCompletedAnnotation parseable as RFC3339; missing is silent no-op, malformed emits RotationAnnotationInvalid Warning. Matches REQ-005.
architecture_findings.patch_then_delete_ordering_justified: rotation_staging.go:63-68 DECISION correctly captures that delete-then-patch would risk data loss if the operator crashes between steps, while patch-then-delete is idempotent because a stale annotation will fail validation on the next reconcile.
architecture_findings.duplicate_staging_ensure_functions: reconcile_fernet.go:216-240 and reconcile_credential.go:236-260 are near-duplicates — see DA1.
architecture_findings.owner_reference_and_watch_wiring: keystone_controller.go's secretToKeystoneMapper (owner-ref branch) correctly routes staging-Secret events back to the Keystone CR, so CronJob PATCHes trigger operator reconciles without a periodic requeue.
architecture_findings.apply_flow_semantically_wrong: See S1 — strategic-merge patch on map[string][]byte merges keys rather than replacing the map. Architecture intent ('atomic swap') is not realized by the chosen mechanism.

good_patterns: Small RBAC blast radius — only get+patch on a narrow resourceNames-scoped staging Secret, no wildcard verbs.
Validation enforced at the operator boundary as defense-in-depth (REQ-006) — a compromised CronJob still cannot push malformed keys to production.
Annotation-gated commit with RFC3339 parseability check decouples 'CronJob finished' from 'CronJob wrote Data'.
Chainsaw regression guard for RBAC split is assertive in both directions: prod must NOT have patch, staging MUST have patch.
Deterministic validation errors via sorted iteration — avoids flaky test matches.
Owner references from staging Secret to Keystone CR keep garbage collection correct on Keystone deletion.
anti_patterns: Strategic-merge patch used on a map field with the assumption of replace semantics (S1). Strategic merge patch's default for untagged maps is merge-by-key, not replace.
Tests that exercise only overlapping-key cases for a merge-vs-replace code path (T1) — the happy-path harness cannot observe the anti-pattern.
Near-verbatim duplication between Fernet and credential sub-reconcilers for staging-Secret ensure (DA1) — invites skew in future fixes.

suggested_improvements: After the S1 fix, add a unit test that seeds production with a key at an index not present in staging, and assert the post-apply production Data equals staging exactly (length and contents). This is the regression guard that locks in the semantic.
Consolidate ensureFernetStagingSecret / ensureCredentialStagingSecret into a single `ensureStagingSecret` helper in rotation_staging.go parameterized by staging name and label value (DA1).

Emit a verbosity-1 log line on the 'staged data without completion annotation' branch so operators can distinguish 'CronJob forgot to annotate' from 'rotation hasn't run yet' without having to instrument the CronJob.
Switch the envtest CronJob simulation from `c.Update` to a strategic-merge PATCH matching the fernet_rotate.sh payload shape, so the end-to-end harness exercises the real write mechanism.

Add a brief note in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md under 'Key Rotation RBAC Split' documenting the field-ownership model for Secret.Data on the production Secret: operator-only, full-object replace semantics, ResourceVersion-driven concurrency. This anchors the S1 fix in the public contract.
next_steps: Fix S1 by switching applyRotationOutput from strategic-merge PATCH to either a GET-then-Update of the production Secret (preferred) or a Server-Side Apply that owns the `.data` field with replace semantics.
Fix T1 by adding the disjoint-index regression case (at minimum one unit test, ideally also one envtest case) that must fail against the current implementation and pass after the S1 fix.
Remove/rewrite the DECISION comment block in rotation_staging.go:63-73 (Q2) and the misleading HappyPath comment in rotation_staging_apply_test.go:292-293 (Q4).
Re-run `go test ./...` and the fernet-rotation + credential-rotation chainsaw tests after the fix; confirm the new regression test fails before the fix and passes after.
Address DA1 (shared staging-Secret helper) in the same PR or a tightly-linked follow-up so the duplication doesn't survive the S1 fix.
